title: Explicit Agent State Machine, Progress, Budgets, Termination, and Cancellation · Language Clinic: Terminal Reasons
summary: A while-true loop stops only when the model chooses text, repeats the same call after unchanged failures, ignores cancellation, and has no terminal reason or partial-result policy. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the terminal reasons increment in bounded observable Python agent loop. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Emit exactly one typed complete, refused, exhausted, failed, cancelled, expired, or escalated terminal reason with partial evidence.
Define observable state change and detect repeated calls, unchanged errors, oscillation, cyclic plans, and declining evidence quality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-bounded-control-loop-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-bounded-control-loop-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_bounded_control_loop_3(step_budget: int = 12, used_steps: int = 7, repeated_call_limit: int = 2, repeated_calls: int = 1, terminal_events: int = 1) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if step_budget < 1 or used_steps < 0 or used_steps > step_budget:
        return False, used_steps, "step-budget"
    if repeated_calls < 0 or repeated_calls > repeated_call_limit:
        return False, repeated_calls, "loop-stall"
    if terminal_events != 1:
        return False, terminal_events, "terminal-ambiguity"
    if used_steps < 1:
        return False, used_steps, "no-progress"
    assert used_steps <= step_budget and terminal_events == 1
    return True, used_steps, "bounded-agent-loop"

        "evaluation61231uo55c",
        "recoveryc55ou16123",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1uo55c)) == 6
    if step_budget < 1 or used_steps < 0 or used_steps > step_budget:
        return False, used_steps, "step-budget"
    if repeated_calls < 0 or repeated_calls > repeated_call_limit:
        return False, repeated_calls, "loop-stall"
    if terminal_events != 1:
        return False, terminal_events, "terminal-ambiguity"
    if used_steps < 1:
        return False, used_steps, "no-progress"
    assert used_steps <= step_budget and terminal_events == 1
    return True, used_steps, "bounded-agent-loop"
